questions.q:
Compound $(X) \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_8 \mathrm{O}$ decolourises Baeyer's reagent. It undergoes hydrolysis on reaction with dil. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ and produces $(Y)$ and $(Z)$. Both $(Y)$ and $(Z)$ give lodoform test positive. Only $(Y)$ gives Tollen's test positive.
Choose the correct statements.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following pairs can be distinguished by using Lucas Test?
questions.solution:
(A)
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Ph}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow[\text { conc. } \mathrm{HCl}]{\text { anh. } \mathrm{nCl} \mathrm{Cl}_2} \mathrm{Ph}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH}) \mathrm{Cl} \text { (immediate) } \\
& \text { (white turbidity) } \\
& \mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow{\text { Lucas reagent }} \mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH}) \mathrm{Cl}
\end{aligned}$
(B) $\mathrm{Ph}-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow[\text { reagent }]{\text { Lucas }} \mathrm{PhCH}_2 \mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH}) \mathrm{Cl}$ (immediate)
(White turbidity)
$\mathrm{Ph}-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow[\text { reagent }]{\text { Lucas }} \text { No reaction under normal condition }$
(C)
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow[\text { reagent }]{\text { Lucas }}\left(\mathrm{CH}_3\right)_2 \mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH}) \mathrm{Cl} \\
& \text { (White turbidity) } \\
& \text { (Moderate rate) } \\
& \mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow[\text { reagent }]{\text { Lucas }} \mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH}) \mathrm{Cl} \\
& \text { (White turbidity) }
\end{aligned}$
(Slow)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Correct statement(s) in cases of $n$ -butanol and $t$ -butanol is (are)
options: both are having equal solubility in water
$t$ -butanol is more soluble in water than $n$ -butanol
boiling point of $t$ -butanol is lower than $n$ -butanol
boiling point of $n$ -butanol is lower than $t$ -butanol
questions.solution: More branching results high solubility and low boiling point.
